claim: Dev mode plaintext password bypass allows fixed admin login without PBKDF2 verification
evidence: Lines 358-361: if (password === import.meta.env.VITE_ADMIN_PASS) { matchedUser = { ...FIXED_ADMIN, id: 'dev-fixed-admin' }; } — plaintext comparison in dev mode. If VITE_DEV_LOGIN_ENABLED leaks to production build, fixed admin can be accessed with plaintext password.
runtimeTrigger: Production build with VITE_DEV_LOGIN_ENABLED=true (misconfiguration) → attacker can login as fixed admin with plaintext password from env var

claim: Unsigned sessions allowed in dev mode without server verification — session tampering possible
evidence: Lines 302-310: if (!session.sig) { if (import.meta.env.PROD) { logout(); return null; } return session; } — dev mode returns unsigned session without HMAC verification. Attacker can tamper with session fields (role, userId) in dev environment.
runtimeTrigger: Dev mode: attacker modifies localStorage session JSON → changes role to 'Super Admin' → getSessionAsync() returns tampered session → elevated privileges

claim: Dev OTP bypass accepts hardcoded '123456' OTP — production builds can be exploited if hostname check is bypassed
evidence: Lines 244-267: const isDev = import.meta.env.DEV && (window.location.hostname === 'localhost' || window.location.hostname === '127.0.0.1'); if (devBypass && otp === '123456') { return success } — hostname check can be bypassed via hosts file or DNS spoofing. If VITE_DEV_OTP_BYPASS leaks to production, attacker can login as any NIK with '123456'.
runtimeTrigger: Production build with VITE_DEV_OTP_BYPASS=true → attacker adds '127.0.0.1 production-domain.com' to hosts file → visits http://127.0.0.1 → OTP bypass active → login as any NIK with '123456'

claim: initUsersStore() sync failure silently falls back to empty cache — users cannot login after sync error
evidence: Lines 161-166: catch (e) { console.error('[auth] Gagal load/sync users:', e); if (_usersCache === null) _usersCache = []; } — if sync fails on first load, cache is set to empty array. All non-fixed-admin users are lost. No retry mechanism.
runtimeTrigger: App starts → Supabase sync fails (network error) → _usersCache = [] → additional admin users cannot login → only fixed admin works

claim: OTP rate limit stored in sessionStorage — attacker can bypass by opening new tab or clearing sessionStorage
evidence: Lines 18-40: sessionStorage.setItem(key, JSON.stringify({ attempts, ts: now })); — rate limit state is per-tab, not global. Attacker can open multiple tabs or clear sessionStorage to reset rate limit counter.
runtimeTrigger: Attacker requests OTP 3 times → rate limit hit → opens new tab → rate limit reset → requests 3 more OTPs → repeats → unlimited OTP requests
